Adjust salt carefully and serve immediately while glossy and loose.

title: Risotto ai Frutti di Mare
description: A practical home-style Risotto ai Frutti di Mare recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1/2 cup dry white wine or additional stock
steps: Keep the seafood stock barely simmering. Scrub shellfish, debeard mussels and cut squid into rings; keep delicate seafood chilled until needed.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a wide pan. Cook mussels or clams with a splash of stock, covered, for 4 to 5 minutes until opened; remove the meat and strain the cooking liquid. Discard unopened shells.
In a clean wide pan, cook the onion in the remaining oil and 1 tablespoon butter for 5 minutes without browning.
Add the rice and toast for 2 minutes, then add wine, if using, and cook until absorbed.
Add the strained shellfish liquid, then hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring regularly.
When the rice has cooked for about 13 minutes, add firm seafood such as squid. Add shrimp and cooked shellfish during the final 3 to 4 minutes.
Remove from the heat when the rice is al dente and the seafood is just cooked. Stir in a little butter and parsley; traditionally, cheese is omitted.
Rest 1 minute, adjust salt and serve immediately.

title: Arancini
description: A practical home-style Arancini recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Use cold, firm risotto that has chilled for at least 4 hours. Divide it into 12 equal portions and keep the mozzarella cubes cold.
Flatten one portion in a damp palm, place a mozzarella cube in the centre and close the rice around it. Compress gently into a tight ball with no exposed cheese.
Arrange flour, beaten eggs and breadcrumbs in three separate shallow dishes.
Coat each ball first in flour, then egg and finally breadcrumbs, pressing the crumbs firmly over the surface.
Chill the breaded arancini for 20 to 30 minutes so the coating sets and the balls hold their shape.
Heat frying oil to 175°C (350°F). Fry in small batches for 4 to 5 minutes, turning, until evenly deep golden.
Drain on a rack rather than paper towel and let stand for 3 minutes; the centre will be extremely hot.
Serve warm while the shell is crisp and the mozzarella is melted.

title: Tiramisu
description: A practical home-style Tiramisu recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Prepare strong espresso, stir in the coffee liqueur if using and cool it completely in a shallow dish.
Whisk the pasteurized egg yolks with half the sugar for 3 to 4 minutes until pale, thick and ribbon-like.
Beat the mascarpone on low speed for about 30 seconds just until smooth, then fold in the yolk mixture without overbeating.
In a clean bowl, beat the egg whites to soft peaks. Add the remaining sugar gradually and beat to glossy medium-stiff peaks.
Fold the whites into the mascarpone mixture in three additions, keeping as much air as possible.
Dip each ladyfinger in the cold espresso for only 1 to 2 seconds per side and arrange a tight layer in the dish.
Spread over half the mascarpone cream, add a second dipped-ladyfinger layer and finish with the remaining cream.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, preferably overnight. Dust generously with cocoa immediately before serving.

title: Panna Cotta
description: A practical home-style Panna Cotta recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Sprinkle the gelatin evenly over the cold milk and leave it undisturbed for 5 to 10 minutes to bloom.
Combine the cream, sugar and vanilla in a saucepan. Heat over medium-low, stirring, until steaming and the sugar dissolves; do not let it boil.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and whisk in the bloomed gelatin until completely dissolved, about 1 minute.
Strain the mixture through a fine sieve to remove any undissolved gelatin and bubbles.
Divide among lightly oiled moulds or serving glasses and cool at room temperature for 20 minutes.
Cover without touching the surface and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, until softly set but still trembling.
To unmould, dip the mould in warm water for 5 seconds and invert onto a chilled plate.
Serve with fresh berries or a lightly tart fruit sauce.

title: Cannoli Siciliani
description: A practical home-style Cannoli Siciliani recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix flour, sugar and a pinch of salt. Rub in the butter, then add the egg and enough grape juice or water to form a firm dough.
Knead for 8 minutes until smooth, wrap and rest for 1 hour at room temperature.
Roll the dough very thin—about 1 mm—and cut into 10 cm rounds. Wrap each around an oiled cannoli tube and seal the overlap with beaten egg.
Heat oil to 180°C (355°F). Fry the shells in batches for 1 to 2 minutes until blistered and deep golden, then drain and remove the tubes when cool enough to handle.
For the filling, press the well-drained ricotta through a sieve and mix with icing sugar until smooth. Fold in chocolate chips.
Chill the filling for 1 hour, then pipe it into both ends of completely cooled shells only shortly before serving.
Dip the ends in chopped pistachios and dust with icing sugar.
Keep unfilled shells airtight; filled cannoli soften quickly.

title: Sfogliatella
description: A practical home-style Sfogliatella recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix flour and salt, add water gradually and knead for 10 to 12 minutes until extremely smooth and firm. Wrap and rest for 1 hour.
Roll the dough into a very thin, long sheet, ideally using a pasta machine. Brush lightly with softened lard or butter as you roll it tightly into a compact cylinder.
Wrap the cylinder and chill for at least 2 hours so the fat firms and the laminated spiral can be sliced cleanly.
For the filling, bring milk to a simmer, whisk in semolina and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until very thick. Cool completely.
Mix the cooled semolina with ricotta, sugar, egg, candied orange peel and cinnamon until smooth; chill for 30 minutes.
Heat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Slice the dough cylinder into 1 cm rounds and press each round outward with your thumbs into a layered cone.
Fill each cone without overpacking, pinch the opening closed and arrange on a lined tray.
Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until crisp and deeply golden. Cool for 15 minutes and dust with powdered sugar.

title: Zabaglione
description: A practical home-style Zabaglione recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Bring 3 cm of water to a bare simmer in a saucepan; the water must not touch the bottom of the mixing bowl.
Whisk egg yolks and sugar in a heatproof bowl for 2 minutes until pale and slightly thick.
Set the bowl over the simmering water and whisk continuously while slowly adding the grape juice.
Continue whisking for 8 to 10 minutes, scraping the sides, until the mixture triples in volume and forms thick ribbons.
Use an instant-read thermometer if possible; the foam should reach at least 71°C (160°F) while remaining smooth.
Remove from the heat and whisk for another minute so the heat distributes evenly.
Spoon immediately over berries for a warm zabaglione, or whisk over an ice bath until cool for a chilled version.
Serve promptly; do not reboil or the yolks may curdle.

title: Semifreddo
description: A practical home-style Semifreddo recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1/2 cup toasted nuts or chocolate, optional
steps: Line a 23 × 13 cm loaf pan with plastic wrap, leaving enough overhang to cover the top.
Whisk the egg yolks with half the sugar over barely simmering water for 5 to 7 minutes until thick, pale and at least 71°C (160°F); cool to room temperature.
Beat the egg whites in a clean bowl to soft peaks. Gradually add the remaining sugar and beat to glossy medium peaks.
Whip the cold cream with vanilla on medium-high speed for 2 to 3 minutes to medium peaks.
Fold the whipped cream into the cooled yolk mixture in three additions, then fold in the meringue gently.
Fold in toasted nuts or chocolate, if using, and spread the mixture into the lined pan. Smooth the top and cover with the plastic overhang.
Freeze for at least 6 hours, preferably overnight, until firm enough to slice.
Unmould and let stand at room temperature for 5 to 8 minutes before cutting with a warm knife.

title: Cassata Siciliana
description: A practical home-style Cassata Siciliana recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Drain the ricotta overnight. Press it through a fine sieve and mix with powdered sugar until very smooth; fold in chocolate chips and chopped candied fruit.
Slice the sponge cake horizontally into thin layers or cut strips to line the chosen mould.
Line the mould with plastic wrap. Roll the marzipan to about 3 mm and use it to line the sides, pressing the joins closed.
Fit a layer of sponge into the base and brush lightly with simple syrup; the cake should be moist, not soaked.
Fill with the ricotta mixture, pressing gently to remove air pockets, then cover with the remaining syrup-brushed sponge.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours so the cake sets and the flavours blend.
Invert onto a serving plate, remove the wrap and coat the top with a thin layer of fondant icing or glaze.
Decorate with candied fruit and chill until 20 minutes before serving.

title: Babà al Rum
description: A practical home-style Babà al Rum recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix the flour, yeast, sugar and salt. Add the warm milk and eggs gradually and beat with a dough hook on medium speed for 8 to 10 minutes until elastic and very sticky.
Add the softened butter a tablespoon at a time, beating until each addition disappears. Continue mixing for 5 minutes until the dough pulls into glossy strands.
Cover and let rise at 24–27°C (75–80°F) for 60 to 90 minutes, until doubled.
Butter baba moulds. Pipe or spoon dough into each mould no more than halfway full and let rise again for 30 to 45 minutes, until near the rim.
Heat the oven to 190°C (375°F). Bake small babas for 15 to 18 minutes or a large one for 25 to 30 minutes, until deep golden and about 93°C (200°F) internally.
Boil the sugar and water for 3 minutes, cool to warm and stir in the rum.
Dip or slowly spoon the warm syrup over the cooled babas until they are saturated but still hold their shape. Drain on a rack for 10 minutes.
Serve with whipped cream and fruit, allowing the babas to rest at least 1 hour after soaking.

title: Crostata
description: A practical home-style Crostata recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk flour, baking powder and salt. Rub or cut in the cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add sugar, lemon zest and eggs and mix only until the dough comes together; do not knead extensively.
Divide the dough into two portions, one about twice the size of the other. Flatten, wrap and chill for 45 to 60 minutes.
Heat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Roll the larger piece to 4 mm thick and line a 23 cm tart pan, pressing it into the corners.
Prick the base lightly and spread the jam evenly, leaving a small border below the rim.
Roll the remaining dough and cut into strips. Arrange a lattice over the jam and press the ends onto the crust edge.
Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the pastry is evenly golden and the jam bubbles gently.
Cool completely in the pan before unmoulding and slicing.

title: Panforte
description: A practical home-style Panforte recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 160°C (325°F). Line the base of a 20 cm springform pan with parchment and grease the sides.
Toast the almonds and hazelnuts for 8 minutes, cool slightly and combine with candied citrus peel.
Whisk flour, cocoa, cinnamon and cloves, then toss this mixture through the warm nuts and fruit until evenly coated.
Heat honey and sugar in a saucepan over medium heat, stirring until dissolved. Boil without stirring until the syrup reaches 116°C (240°F).
Immediately pour the hot syrup over the nut mixture and stir rapidly with a sturdy spoon; it will become very thick within seconds.
Press firmly and evenly into the prepared pan using a damp or lightly oiled spatula.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes; the centre should remain slightly soft because panforte firms as it cools.
Cool completely, unmould and dust with powdered sugar. Wrap and rest for a day before slicing thinly for the best texture.

title: Zeppole
description: A practical home-style Zeppole recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Dissolve the yeast and sugar in the warm water and leave for 5 minutes until slightly foamy.
Mix flour and salt, add the yeast water and lemon zest and beat vigorously with a wooden spoon for 4 to 5 minutes into a sticky, elastic batter.
Cover and let rise at warm room temperature for 60 to 90 minutes, until doubled and full of bubbles.
Heat 7 cm of frying oil to 175°C (350°F). Keep a small bowl of water nearby for wetting the spoon and fingers.
Drop tablespoon-size portions of batter into the oil without crowding the pan.
Fry for 3 to 4 minutes, turning several times, until puffed and evenly deep golden; maintain the oil between 170 and 180°C (340–355°F).
Drain on a rack or paper towel and check one zeppola to ensure the centre is cooked rather than gummy.
Dust generously with powdered sugar and serve warm.

title: Gelato
description: A practical home-style Gelato recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine milk, cream, half the sugar and salt in a saucepan. Heat over medium-low until steaming but not boiling.
Whisk the egg yolks with the remaining sugar for 2 minutes until pale.
Slowly whisk about one-third of the hot milk into the yolks, then pour the tempered yolks back into the saucepan.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly with a spatula, until the custard reaches 82–84°C (180–183°F) and coats the spatula; do not boil.
Strain into a clean bowl, add vanilla and cool over an ice bath. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Churn in an ice-cream machine according to its instructions, usually 20 to 30 minutes, until softly frozen and dense.
Pack into a shallow container, press parchment against the surface and freeze for 2 to 3 hours to firm.
Let stand at room temperature for 5 minutes before scooping; gelato is served slightly warmer than standard ice cream.

title: Affogato
description: A practical home-style Affogato recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Chill four small serving glasses or cups so the gelato does not melt before the espresso is ready.
Place one firm scoop of vanilla gelato in each chilled glass.
Pull four fresh espresso shots immediately before serving; each should be hot and aromatic with a visible crema.
Carry the gelato and espresso to the table separately if possible.
Pour one hot espresso shot directly over each scoop only at the moment of serving.
Scatter shaved dark chocolate over the top.
Serve with a small spoon so the diner can alternate between the melting gelato and coffee.
Do not prepare in advance; the contrast between very cold gelato and hot espresso defines the dessert.

title: Torrone
description: A practical home-style Torrone recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Edible wafer paper or parchment for lining
steps: Toast the almonds and pistachios at 160°C (325°F) for 8 to 10 minutes and keep them warm. Line a pan with edible wafer paper or parchment.
Combine honey and sugar syrup ingredients in separate saucepans if using the traditional two-syrup method; heat the honey gently and cook the sugar syrup to 145°C (293°F).
Beat the egg whites with salt to soft peaks in a heatproof mixer bowl.
With the mixer running on low, slowly stream in the hot honey, then the hot sugar syrup, avoiding the whisk and bowl sides.
Increase to medium speed and beat for 6 to 10 minutes until the nougat is thick, glossy and begins to pull away from the bowl.
Fold in the warm toasted nuts and citrus zest quickly with a sturdy spatula.
Press the mixture into the lined pan, cover with a second wafer sheet or parchment and flatten firmly to an even thickness.
Cool at room temperature for at least 6 hours, then cut with a lightly oiled heavy knife and wrap airtight.

title: Ricciarelli
description: A practical home-style Ricciarelli recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk the almond flour, powdered sugar, orange zest and baking powder until evenly combined and free of lumps.
Beat the egg whites only to soft, foamy peaks; they should not become dry.
Fold the egg whites and almond extract into the almond mixture to make a soft, sticky dough.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, so the dough hydrates and the flavour develops.
Heat the oven to 160°C (325°F) and line two trays with parchment.
Shape tablespoon portions into plump diamonds or ovals, coat generously in powdered sugar and arrange with space between them.
Bake for 14 to 17 minutes until the surfaces crack and the edges are set while the centres remain soft and pale.
Cool completely on the tray before moving; they firm as they cool.

title: Torta Caprese
description: A practical home-style Torta Caprese recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 170°C (340°F). Grease a 23 cm springform pan and line the base with parchment.
Melt the chocolate and butter together over barely simmering water or in short microwave bursts; stir smooth and cool for 10 minutes.
Beat the egg yolks with half the sugar for 3 to 4 minutes until pale and thick, then mix in vanilla and the cooled chocolate mixture.
Fold in the almond flour and salt until no dry patches remain.
In a clean bowl, beat the egg whites to soft peaks, gradually add the remaining sugar and beat to glossy medium peaks.
Fold the meringue into the chocolate batter in three additions, preserving the air.
Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the top is set and cracked but a skewer inserted near the centre carries moist crumbs.
Cool completely before unmoulding and dusting with powdered sugar.

title: Struffoli
description: A practical home-style Struffoli recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Mix flour, sugar, baking powder and lemon zest. Add eggs and melted butter and knead for 5 to 7 minutes into a smooth, firm dough.
Wrap the dough and rest it for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Divide into pieces, roll each into a rope about 1 cm thick and cut into small bean-size nuggets; dust lightly with flour and shake off excess.
Heat frying oil to 175°C (350°F). Fry the dough pieces in small batches for 2 to 3 minutes until puffed and golden.
Drain thoroughly on a rack or paper towel.
Warm the honey in a large saucepan over low heat until fluid but not boiling. Add the fried pieces and fold for 1 to 2 minutes until evenly coated.
Pile the honeyed struffoli into a wreath or mound on a serving plate while still warm.
Scatter with coloured sprinkles and let set for at least 30 minutes before serving.

title: Amaretti
description: A practical home-style Amaretti recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 165°C (330°F) and line two trays with parchment.
Whisk the almond flour, sugar and salt until evenly mixed and lump-free.
Beat the egg whites to soft peaks, then fold in the almond extract.
Fold the whites into the almond mixture to form a thick, sticky paste; rest for 10 minutes.
With damp hands, roll tablespoon portions into balls and coat them generously in powdered sugar.
Arrange 4 cm apart and press very lightly so they sit securely without flattening completely.
Bake for 15 to 18 minutes until cracked and lightly golden at the edges while still soft in the centre.
Cool on the tray for 10 minutes before transferring to a rack.

title: Castagnaccio
description: A practical home-style Castagnaccio recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 190°C (375°F). Grease a shallow 28 cm pan with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Soak the raisins in warm water for 10 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
Sift the chestnut flour and salt into a bowl. Whisk in the water gradually until the batter is smooth and similar to thin pancake batter.
Whisk in 1 tablespoon olive oil and half the raisins and pine nuts.
Pour into the prepared pan to a depth of about 1 cm and smooth the surface.
Scatter over the remaining raisins, pine nuts and rosemary and drizzle with the final tablespoon olive oil.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the surface is dry and cracked and the edges pull slightly from the pan.
Cool to room temperature before slicing; the interior should remain dense and moist, not cake-like.

title: Biscotti di Prato
description: A practical home-style Biscotti di Prato recipe from Italian cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 175°C (350°F) and line a baking tray with parchment. Toast the whole almonds for 6 minutes and cool.
Whisk flour, baking powder and salt. In another bowl, beat eggs and sugar for 2 to 3 minutes until slightly thick, then add vanilla and orange zest.
Fold the dry ingredients into the egg mixture, then work in the whole almonds. The dough will be sticky and firm.
With damp hands, shape into two logs about 30 cm long and 5 cm wide, leaving space between them.
Bake for 22 to 27 minutes until lightly golden and firm to the touch. Cool for 10 minutes only.
Reduce the oven to 150°C (300°F). Slice the warm logs diagonally into 1.5 cm pieces with a serrated knife.
Lay the slices cut-side down and bake for 10 minutes, turn and bake another 8 to 10 minutes until dry and crisp.
Cool completely on a rack before storing airtight.

title: Macaroni and Cheese
description: A practical home-style Macaroni and Cheese recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat the oven to 190°C (375°F). Butter a 23 × 33 cm baking dish. Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil and season it generously with salt.
Boil the macaroni for 2 minutes less than the package's al dente time. Drain well without rinsing; the pasta should still feel firm because it will finish cooking in the oven.
Melt 4 tablespoons butter in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 2 minutes, whisking constantly, until the roux smells lightly toasted but has not browned.

Pour in the warm milk in three additions, whisking smooth after each addition. Add evaporated milk, mustard, garlic powder, paprika and nutmeg. Simmer gently for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring, until the sauce coats the back of a spoon.

Remove the pan from the heat. Add the cheddar and Monterey Jack by handfuls, stirring until each addition melts before adding the next. Season with salt and black pepper; do not boil after adding the cheese or the sauce may become grainy.
Fold the drained macaroni into the cheese sauce and transfer it to the prepared dish. Mix the panko with 2 tablespoons melted butter and scatter it evenly over the surface.
Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until bubbling around the edges and lightly browned on top. Rest for 10 minutes before serving so the sauce thickens slightly.

title: Shepherd’s Pie—American Style
description: A practical home-style Shepherd’s Pie—American Style recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 kg russet or Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and cubed
steps: Place the potatoes in a saucepan, cover with cold salted water and bring to a boil. Reduce to a steady simmer and cook for 15 to 18 minutes, until a knife slides through easily.

Drain the potatoes thoroughly and return them to the hot pan for 1 minute to steam off excess moisture. Mash with butter and warm milk, then beat in the egg yolk for 30 seconds until smooth. Season and set aside.
Heat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Warm the oil in a deep oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the ground beef for 7 to 9 minutes, breaking it into small pieces; drain excess fat if necessary.
Add onion, carrots and celery. Cook over medium heat for 7 minutes until softened, then add garlic, tomato paste and thyme and cook for 1 minute.
Sprinkle the flour over the filling and stir for 1 minute. Add Worcestershire sauce and stock, scraping up the browned bits. Simmer for 5 to 7 minutes until thick enough to hold a trail when stirred.
Fold in the peas, taste for seasoning and level the filling. Spoon the mashed potatoes over it and spread to the edges, making ridges with a fork so the top browns evenly.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until the filling bubbles and the potato ridges are golden. Rest for 10 minutes before portioning.

title: Chicken Pot Pie
description: A practical home-style Chicken Pot Pie recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 600 g boneless chicken thighs, cut into 2 cm pieces
1 sheet refrigerated pie pastry for the base
1 sheet refrigerated pie pastry for the top
steps: Heat the oven to 210°C (410°F). Fit one pastry sheet into a 23 cm pie dish, trim the edge and refrigerate while preparing the filling.
Season the chicken. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a wide pan over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring, until lightly browned but not fully cooked. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining butter, onion, carrots and celery to the pan. Cook over medium heat for 7 to 8 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.
Stir in flour, thyme and sage and cook for 2 minutes. Gradually whisk in the stock, then the milk. Simmer for 5 minutes until the gravy is smooth and thick.
Return the chicken and its juices to the pan, add the peas and simmer for 3 minutes. The chicken should reach 74°C (165°F). Cool the filling for 10 minutes so it does not melt the pastry.
Spoon the filling into the chilled shell. Cover with the second pastry sheet, crimp the edges and cut four steam vents. Brush the top with egg wash.
Bake for 20 minutes, reduce the oven to 190°C (375°F), and continue for 20 to 25 minutes until the crust is deep golden and the filling is bubbling through the vents. Rest 15 minutes before cutting.

title: Tuna Casserole
description: A practical home-style Tuna Casserole recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2 cans tuna in water, drained well
1 cup crushed potato chips or buttered breadcrumbs
Salt, black pepper and 1/2 tsp dried dill
steps: Heat the oven to 190°C (375°F) and butter a 23 × 33 cm casserole dish. Boil the noodles in salted water for 2 minutes less than the package directions, then drain.
Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the mushrooms for 5 minutes until browned, then add the onion and cook 3 minutes more.
Add the remaining butter. When melted, stir in the flour and cook for 90 seconds. Slowly whisk in the warm milk and stock.
Add mustard, dill, salt and pepper. Simmer gently for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring, until the sauce is thick but still pourable.
Remove from the heat and fold in tuna, peas, cheddar and noodles, taking care not to crush the tuna completely. Transfer to the casserole dish.
Scatter crushed chips or buttered breadcrumbs evenly over the surface. Bake for 22 to 28 minutes until the edges bubble and the topping is crisp.
Let the casserole stand for 8 minutes before serving so the sauce settles around the noodles.

title: Beef Stroganoff
description: A practical home-style Beef Stroganoff recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 700 g beef sirloin, sliced across the grain into thin strips
3/4 cup full-fat sour cream, at room temperature
steps: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil for the noodles. Pat the beef completely dry and season it with salt and pepper.

Heat a wide skillet over high heat until very hot. Add the oil and sear the beef in two batches for 60 to 90 seconds per side, just until browned. Transfer it to a plate; overcooking now will make it tough.
Lower the heat to medium and melt 2 tablespoons butter. Add mushrooms in a single layer and cook for 5 minutes before stirring, then add the onion and cook for another 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds. Sprinkle the flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute, scraping the browned fond from the skillet.
Gradually stir in the beef stock, mustard and Worcestershire sauce. Simmer for 5 to 7 minutes until reduced to a light gravy.
Cook the noodles until tender, drain and toss with the remaining tablespoon of butter.

Reduce the skillet to its lowest heat. Temper the sour cream with a few spoonfuls of hot gravy, then stir it into the sauce without boiling. Return the beef and its juices for 1 to 2 minutes, just to heat through.
Adjust seasoning, sprinkle with parsley and spoon over the hot buttered noodles.

title: Sloppy Joe
description: A practical home-style Sloppy Joe recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and cook for 7 to 8 minutes, breaking it into small crumbles, until no pink remains. Spoon off excess fat.
Add the onion and bell pepper and cook over medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes until softened. Stir in garlic and tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato sauce, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, mustard, paprika and stock. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook uncovered over medium-low heat for 15 to 20 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until the mixture is thick enough to mound on a spoon rather than run off it.
Taste and balance the sauce with salt, pepper, a little extra mustard for tang or a pinch of sugar if the tomatoes are sharp.
Split and toast the buns cut-side down in a dry skillet for 1 to 2 minutes so they resist becoming soggy.
Spoon the hot beef mixture generously onto the bottom buns and close with the tops. Serve immediately.

title: Corn Dogs
description: A practical home-style Corn Dogs recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2 tbsp neutral oil for the batter
steps: Insert a skewer lengthwise into each hot dog, leaving enough handle to hold. Pat the hot dogs very dry and dust them lightly with flour so the batter adheres.
Heat 7 to 8 cm of frying oil in a deep pot to 175°C (350°F). Keep the temperature between 170 and 180°C throughout frying.
Whisk cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder and salt in a bowl. In a second bowl beat the egg, buttermilk and 2 tablespoons oil for 30 seconds.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and whisk just until smooth, about 20 to 30 seconds. Transfer the thick batter to a tall narrow glass.
Dip one hot dog at a time straight down into the batter, turning to coat completely. Lift slowly and let only the excess tip drip off.
Lower into the hot oil and fry 2 or 3 at a time for 3 to 4 minutes, turning with tongs, until evenly deep golden. The centre should be piping hot, at least 74°C (165°F).
Drain on a wire rack rather than paper towels to keep the coating crisp. Serve immediately with mustard and ketchup.

title: Stuffed Peppers
description: A practical home-style Stuffed Peppers recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 190°C (375°F). Slice the tops from the peppers, remove the seeds and membranes, and stand the peppers upright in a baking dish.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Blanch the peppers for 4 minutes, drain upside down and let them cool slightly; this ensures the walls become tender without collapsing.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the beef for 6 to 8 minutes, breaking it up, then drain excess fat.
Add onion and cook for 4 minutes. Stir in garlic, oregano and paprika for 30 seconds, then add diced tomatoes, cooked rice and half the tomato sauce.
Simmer the filling for 3 to 4 minutes, season well and remove from heat. Fold in half the cheese.
Spoon the remaining tomato sauce into the baking dish. Pack the filling into the peppers without compressing it too tightly and cover the dish with foil.
Bake for 30 minutes, uncover, top with the remaining cheese and bake 12 to 15 minutes more until the peppers are tender and the filling reaches 74°C (165°F). Rest 5 minutes before serving.

title: Baked Beans
description: A practical home-style Baked Beans recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups water or bean cooking liquid
steps: Heat the oven to 160°C (325°F). Cook the diced beef bacon in a Dutch oven over medium heat for 5 to 7 minutes until its edges brown.
Add the onion and cook in the rendered fat for 5 minutes until translucent. Stir in ketchup, molasses, brown sugar, mustard, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce and paprika.
Add the beans and 1 cup of the water or bean liquid. Stir gently so the beans stay intact and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover and bake for 1 hour. Remove the lid, stir carefully and add more liquid if the beans look dry.
Continue baking uncovered for 45 to 60 minutes, until the sauce is dark, glossy and thick enough to cling to the beans.
Taste before adding salt because the bacon and Worcestershire sauce are already seasoned. Add black pepper and a little vinegar if the sauce needs brightness.
Rest for 15 minutes before serving; the sauce will continue thickening as it cools.

title: Tater Tot Casserole
description: A practical home-style Tater Tot Casserole recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Brown the ground beef in a 30 cm oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat for 7 minutes, breaking it into small pieces. Transfer it to a bowl.
Melt the butter in the skillet. Add onion and mushrooms and cook for 7 to 8 minutes until the mushrooms brown and release their moisture.
Add garlic for 30 seconds, then sprinkle in flour and stir for 1 minute. Slowly whisk in the stock and milk until smooth.
Add Worcestershire sauce and simmer for 4 minutes until the gravy thickens. Return the beef and add the frozen vegetables. Season and spread the filling level.
Sprinkle half the cheddar over the filling. Arrange the frozen tater tots in a tight single layer over the entire surface; keeping them frozen helps them crisp.
Bake uncovered for 30 to 35 minutes until the tots are deeply golden and the filling bubbles around the edges.
Scatter over the remaining cheddar and bake 5 minutes more. Rest for 10 minutes before serving.

title: Philly Cheesesteak
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Philly Cheesesteak recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 700 g rib-eye or sirloin, partially frozen and shaved very thin
1 green bell pepper, thinly sliced, optional
8 slices provolone or 1 cup cheese sauce
steps: Place the beef in the freezer for 30 minutes so it firms up, then slice it across the grain as thinly as possible. Separate the slices so they cook quickly.

Heat a flat griddle or large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add 1 tablespoon oil and cook the onion for 6 to 8 minutes until soft and browned at the edges. Add peppers and mushrooms, if using, and cook until tender. Move the vegetables to one side.
Increase the heat to high, add the remaining oil and spread the shaved beef in a thin layer. Leave it undisturbed for 45 seconds, then chop and turn it with two spatulas.
Season with salt and pepper and cook only 2 to 3 minutes total, until browned but still juicy. Mix the onions and vegetables through the beef.
Divide the mixture into four mounds shaped to fit the rolls. Lay provolone over each mound, add 1 tablespoon water beside the meat and cover for 30 to 45 seconds so the steam melts the cheese.
Split the rolls without cutting all the way through. Butter the cut sides and toast them on the griddle for 1 to 2 minutes.
Slide each cheesy beef mound directly into a toasted roll and serve immediately.

title: Chicago Deep-Dish Pizza
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Chicago Deep-Dish Pizza recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 400 g cooked Italian-style beef sausage, crumbled
800 g crushed tomatoes, drained for 10 minutes
steps: Mix flour, cornmeal, yeast, salt and sugar in a stand-mixer bowl. Add warm water, olive oil and melted butter. Knead with the dough hook on medium-low speed for 6 to 7 minutes until smooth and supple.
Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover and rise at room temperature for 75 to 90 minutes, until nearly doubled. Punch down gently and rest for 10 minutes.
Heat the oven to 220°C (425°F). Brush a 30 cm deep-dish pan generously with oil. Stretch the dough across the base and 4 to 5 cm up the sides; avoid making the bottom too thick.
Layer the mozzarella directly over the dough. Add the cooked sausage, then spread the drained crushed tomatoes over the top. Sprinkle with garlic, oregano, chilli flakes and Parmesan.
Bake on the lower oven rack for 30 to 35 minutes. If the rim browns too quickly, cover only the edge with foil. The bottom should be crisp and the sauce bubbling.
Rest the pizza in the pan for 10 minutes, then loosen the edge with a thin spatula and transfer it to a board.
Wait another 5 minutes before cutting into wedges so the layers remain distinct.

title: Detroit-Style Pizza
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Detroit-Style Pizza recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 350 g brick cheese or low-moisture mozzarella, cubed
steps: Combine flour, yeast and salt. Add the warm water and mix with a dough hook on low speed for 2 minutes, then medium speed for 6 minutes, until the dough is very soft, elastic and slightly sticky.
Oil a dark 23 × 33 cm metal pan generously. Place the dough in the pan, turn it to coat, cover and rest for 15 minutes.
Stretch the relaxed dough toward the corners. If it springs back, wait another 10 minutes and stretch again. Cover and rise for 45 to 60 minutes until puffy.
Heat the oven to 260°C (500°F) with a rack in the lowest third. Press out any large air bubbles without deflating the whole dough.
Scatter the cheese all the way to the pan's edges so it caramelizes against the metal. Arrange pepperoni over the cheese, then spoon the sauce in three long stripes across the pizza.
Bake for 13 to 17 minutes, rotating once, until the top is browned and the cheese forms a dark crisp border around the pan.
Run a metal spatula around the edges immediately. Lift the pizza onto a board, rest for 3 minutes and cut into squares.

title: Buffalo Wings
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Buffalo Wings recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1.5 kg chicken wings, split and tips removed
Celery sticks and blue cheese dressing for serving
steps: Pat the wings exceptionally dry. Toss them with baking powder, salt, garlic powder and black pepper until lightly and evenly coated.
Arrange the wings skin-side up on a wire rack over a sheet pan, leaving space between them. Refrigerate uncovered for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, to dry the skin.
Heat the oven to 230°C (450°F). Bake the wings for 20 minutes, turn each piece, then bake 20 to 25 minutes more until deeply browned and crisp. They must reach at least 74°C (165°F) internally.
While the wings bake, melt the butter over low heat. Whisk in hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce and garlic powder until emulsified; do not boil.
Transfer the piping-hot wings to a large bowl. Pour over enough sauce to coat and toss vigorously for 20 to 30 seconds.
For a drier finish, return the sauced wings to the rack for 3 minutes in the oven. For classic wet wings, serve directly from the bowl.
Serve immediately with celery sticks and blue cheese dressing.

title: Cincinnati Chili
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Cincinnati Chili recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Kidney beans, diced onion and finely grated cheddar for serving
steps: Place the raw ground beef and cold water in a heavy pot. Whisk or mash vigorously for 2 to 3 minutes until the beef disperses into very fine particles; this creates the characteristic smooth texture.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer. Skim off foam and excess fat during the first 10 minutes.
Add onion, garlic, tomato sauce, chilli powder, cocoa, cinnamon, cumin, allspice, cloves, bay leaf, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar and salt.
Simmer uncovered on the lowest heat for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, stirring every 15 minutes. The chilli should reduce to a loose, finely textured sauce rather than a thick Texas-style stew.
Remove the bay leaf and chill the pot overnight when possible. Lift off the solidified fat, then reheat gently the next day for the most developed flavour.
Boil the spaghetti until tender and drain well. Spoon chilli over the pasta.
Serve as a two-way with chilli, three-way with cheddar, four-way with diced onion, or five-way with both onion and kidney beans.

title: Hawaiian Poke Bowl
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Hawaiian Poke Bowl recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 500 g sashimi-grade ahi tuna, cut into 2 cm cubes
3 cups cooked short-grain rice, cooled slightly
1/2 tsp chilli flakes or finely chopped chilli
steps: Buy tuna specifically sold for raw consumption and keep it below 5°C (41°F). Use a clean knife and board reserved for ready-to-eat food.
Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, ginger and chilli in a chilled bowl.
Add the tuna cubes, spring onion and sweet onion. Fold gently for 20 seconds so the fish is coated without breaking.
Cover and refrigerate for 10 to 15 minutes only. A long marinade will cure the tuna and change its tender texture.
Divide the warm or room-temperature rice among four bowls. Arrange cucumber and avocado around the rice.
Spoon the marinated tuna and its dressing over the bowls. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and seaweed.
Serve immediately and do not leave the raw fish at room temperature for more than 2 hours.

title: Hot Brown
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Hot Brown recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 cup grated Pecorino Romano or Parmesan
steps: Heat the broiler to high and arrange four ovenproof plates or shallow gratin dishes on a sheet pan.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 2 minutes without browning.
Add the warm milk gradually while whisking. Simmer for 5 to 6 minutes until thick enough to coat a spoon, then stir in half the cheese, nutmeg, salt and pepper.
Place two toast slices in each dish and cover generously with sliced turkey. Ladle the hot cheese sauce over the turkey so it runs to the edges.
Add two tomato slices to each serving and sprinkle with the remaining cheese.
Broil 10 to 15 cm from the heat for 3 to 5 minutes, watching constantly, until the sauce bubbles and develops browned spots.
Top each sandwich with two strips of crisp beef bacon, sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately on the hot dish.

title: Monte Cristo Sandwich
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Monte Cristo Sandwich recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Powdered sugar and berry preserves for serving
steps: Spread a thin layer of Dijon mustard on one side of each bread slice. Build four sandwiches with turkey, beef ham and Swiss cheese, keeping the fillings inside the bread edges.
Press the sandwiches firmly and trim the crusts if a neat restaurant-style shape is desired. Refrigerate for 10 minutes so the layers hold together.
Whisk eggs, milk, salt and nutmeg in a shallow dish for 45 seconds until fully combined.
Melt half the butter in a broad skillet over medium-low heat. Dip two sandwiches quickly on all sides in the egg mixture; do not soak them until soggy.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes per side and briefly stand each sandwich on its edges, until the bread is evenly golden and the cheese has melted. Repeat with the remaining butter and sandwiches.
Transfer to a rack for 2 minutes so the coating stays crisp. Cut each sandwich diagonally.
Dust lightly with powdered sugar and serve hot with berry preserves on the side.

title: Reuben Sandwich
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Reuben Sandwich recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 500 g thinly sliced cooked corned beef
steps: Warm the corned beef gently in a covered skillet with 2 tablespoons water for 2 to 3 minutes; do not fry it dry.
Spread Russian dressing on one side of every rye slice. Layer Swiss cheese, warm corned beef and well-drained sauerkraut on four slices, then add another cheese slice.
Close the sandwiches with the remaining bread, dressing-side inward. Butter the outside surfaces evenly to the edges.
Heat a heavy skillet or griddle over medium-low heat. Place the sandwiches in the pan and cover loosely for 2 minutes to help the cheese melt.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes per side, pressing lightly with a spatula, until the rye is crisp and deeply browned and the cheese is fully melted.
Rest on a rack for 1 minute before slicing; this keeps steam from softening the underside.
Serve hot with extra dressing and pickles.

title: Cuban Sandwich
mealType: Regional American Dishes, Pizza and Sandwiches
description: A practical home-style Cuban Sandwich recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 4 Cuban rolls or soft baguette sections
2 large dill pickles, thinly sliced lengthwise
steps: Split the rolls lengthwise without cutting completely through. Spread mustard over both cut sides.
Layer the bottom of each roll with Swiss cheese, beef ham, warm sliced roast pork and pickles. Add a second layer of cheese so melted cheese helps bind the sandwich.
Close the rolls and press them firmly with your hands. Spread butter over the outside of each sandwich.
Heat a sandwich press, plancha or cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Place the sandwiches on the hot surface.
Press with the lid or weight with a second heavy skillet. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes per side until the bread is crisp, flattened and golden and the cheese has melted.
The pork filling should be reheated to 74°C (165°F). Adjust the heat lower if the bread browns before the centre becomes hot.
Cut each sandwich diagonally and serve at once.

title: Shrimp and Grits
description: A practical home-style Shrimp and Grits recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 4 cups water or light chicken stock
700 g large shrimp, peeled and deveined
steps: Bring the water or stock to a boil in a heavy saucepan. Whisk in the grits slowly to prevent lumps, then reduce to the lowest heat.
Cook the grits for 30 to 40 minutes, stirring from the bottom every 4 to 5 minutes, until creamy and no hard grains remain. Add the milk during the final 10 minutes.
Remove from the heat and stir in butter and cheddar. Season and cover to keep warm; loosen with hot milk if they become too thick.
Pat the shrimp dry and season with paprika, cayenne, salt and pepper. Cook the diced beef bacon in a skillet over medium heat until crisp, then remove it, leaving 1 tablespoon fat.
Increase the skillet to medium-high. Add the shrimp in one layer and cook 90 seconds on the first side and about 60 seconds on the second, until opaque and 63°C (145°F).
Add garlic for 20 seconds, return the bacon and pour in lemon juice with 2 tablespoons water. Toss to make a light pan sauce.
Spoon hot grits into bowls, top with shrimp and pan juices, and finish with spring onions.

title: Jambalaya
description: A practical home-style Jambalaya recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Salt and hot sauce to taste
steps: Season the chicken with half the Cajun seasoning. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown the chicken for 5 to 6 minutes. Remove it to a plate.
Add the sausage and cook for 3 minutes until browned. Add onion, bell pepper and celery and cook for 6 to 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in garlic, remaining Cajun seasoning, thyme and paprika for 30 seconds. Add tomatoes and scrape the browned bits from the bottom.
Stir in the rice until every grain is coated, then add stock, bay leaves, chicken and sausage. Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce to the lowest heat and cook undisturbed for 18 minutes. Do not stir or the rice can become gummy.
Scatter the shrimp over the rice, cover again and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until the shrimp are opaque and the rice is tender.
Remove from heat and rest covered for 10 minutes. Discard bay leaves, fluff gently, adjust salt and hot sauce, and finish with spring onions.

title: Gumbo
description: A practical home-style Gumbo recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: Cooked white rice and sliced spring onions
steps: Heat the oil in a heavy Dutch oven over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook, stirring continuously with a flat wooden spoon, for 25 to 35 minutes until the roux reaches the colour of dark milk chocolate. If black specks appear, discard it and start again.
Immediately add onion, bell pepper and celery to stop the roux from darkening. Cook for 7 minutes, stirring, then add garlic for 30 seconds.
Add chicken and sausage and stir them through the roux for 5 minutes. Pour in the hot stock gradually, stirring smooth after each addition.
Add okra, bay leaves, thyme, paprika, cayenne, salt and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a very gentle simmer.
Cook uncovered for 60 to 75 minutes, stirring the bottom every 10 minutes, until the chicken is tender and the gumbo has a silky body.
Skim excess fat, remove bay leaves and adjust seasoning. Turn off the heat and stir in filé powder only after the gumbo stops boiling, if using.
Ladle over cooked white rice and finish with spring onions.

title: Red Beans and Rice
description: A practical home-style Red Beans and Rice recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 500 g dried red kidney beans, soaked overnight
Salt, black pepper and hot sauce
steps: Drain and rinse the soaked beans. Heat oil in a heavy pot over medium heat and brown the sausage for 4 to 5 minutes; transfer half to a plate.
Add onion, bell pepper and celery to the pot and cook for 7 minutes. Stir in garlic, thyme, paprika and cayenne for 30 seconds.
Add beans, bay leaves, water or stock and all the sausage. Bring to a full boil for 10 minutes, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cook partially covered for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, stirring every 20 minutes and adding hot water as needed, until the beans are completely tender.
Mash about one cup of beans against the side of the pot and stir them back in. Simmer uncovered for another 15 minutes until the liquid becomes creamy.
Add salt only after the beans are nearly tender, then adjust pepper and hot sauce. Remove bay leaves.
Serve the beans over hot white rice; the mixture should be creamy enough to flow around the rice but not watery.

title: Hoppin’ John
description: A practical home-style Hoppin’ John recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 400 g dried black-eyed peas, soaked overnight
200 g smoked beef sausage or beef bacon, diced
Salt, black pepper and hot sauce
steps: Drain the soaked black-eyed peas. Cook the sausage or beef bacon in a heavy pot over medium heat for 5 minutes until browned.
Add onion, celery and bell pepper and cook for 6 to 7 minutes. Add garlic, thyme and paprika and stir for 30 seconds.
Add the peas, bay leaf and stock. Bring to a boil, then simmer partially covered for 35 to 50 minutes until the peas are tender but still hold their shape.
Measure 3 cups of the hot cooking liquid into a separate saucepan, adding water if needed. Bring it to a boil and stir in the rice.
Cover, reduce to the lowest heat and cook the rice for 16 to 18 minutes. Remove from heat and rest covered for 10 minutes.
Drain any excess liquid from the peas and remove the bay leaf. Fold the peas and savoury vegetables gently through the rice.
Season with salt, black pepper and hot sauce, and finish with spring onions.

title: Chicken and Dumplings
description: A practical home-style Chicken and Dumplings recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Season the chicken and brown it skin-side down for 5 minutes, then turn and cook 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add onion, carrots and celery and cook for 6 minutes. Add garlic for 30 seconds, then pour in the stock and scrape the pot.
Return the chicken with thyme and bay leaf. Simmer partially covered for 30 to 35 minutes until the chicken reaches 74°C (165°F) and pulls easily from the bone.
Remove the chicken, discard skin and bones, and shred the meat. Return it to the pot with the milk and keep the broth at a gentle simmer.
For the dumplings, whisk flour, baking powder and salt. Rub in the cold butter until pea-size pieces remain, then fold in buttermilk and parsley just until a shaggy dough forms; do not overmix.
Drop heaped tablespoons of dough over the simmering stew, leaving a little space between them. Cover tightly and cook on low for 15 minutes without lifting the lid.
Check one dumpling; its centre should be dry and fluffy, not doughy. Season the stew with black pepper and serve immediately.

title: Biscuits and Gravy
description: A practical home-style Biscuits and Gravy recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 230°C (450°F). Whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt in a large bowl.
Cut in the cold butter with fingertips or a pastry cutter until flat pea-size pieces remain. Pour in the cold buttermilk and fold only 8 to 10 times, just until no dry flour remains.
Turn the dough onto a floured surface, pat it into a rectangle, fold it in thirds and repeat twice to create layers. Pat to 2.5 cm thick and cut straight down with a floured cutter.
Place biscuits close together on a lined tray and bake for 12 to 15 minutes until tall and golden. Do not twist the cutter, which seals the edges.
While they bake, crumble the sausage into a skillet and cook over medium heat for 7 to 8 minutes. Leave about 3 tablespoons rendered fat in the pan.
Sprinkle flour over the sausage and cook for 2 minutes. Add warm milk gradually, stirring constantly, then simmer for 5 to 7 minutes until thick and creamy.
Season with sage, plenty of black pepper and salt. Split the hot biscuits and spoon the sausage gravy over them.

title: Country Ham
description: A practical home-style Country Ham recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 whole cured country-style beef ham, 3 to 4 kg
steps: Scrub the cured ham under cool running water to remove surface salt and any harmless curing bloom. Place it in a non-reactive container and cover with cold water.
Refrigerate and soak for 12 to 24 hours, changing the water two or three times. A very heavily cured ham may need up to 36 hours.
Place the ham in a large pot, cover with fresh water and add bay leaves, peppercorns and onion. Bring just to a simmer; do not boil vigorously.
Cook at 85 to 90°C (185 to 195°F) for about 20 minutes per 500 g, until the centre reaches 63°C (145°F). Let it cool in the cooking liquid for 30 minutes.
Heat the oven to 190°C (375°F). Remove the rind, leaving a thin fat layer, and score the fat in shallow diamonds.
Mix brown sugar, mustard, vinegar and cloves. Spread over the ham and bake uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes until the glaze bubbles and caramelizes.
Rest for 20 minutes before carving into very thin slices across the grain.

title: Collard Greens
description: A practical home-style Collard Greens recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 200 g smoked beef bacon or smoked turkey, diced
steps: Fill a clean sink or large basin with cold water. Swish the collard leaves thoroughly, lift them out and repeat until no grit remains.
Remove the tough centre stems. Stack the leaves, roll them tightly and cut crosswise into 2 cm ribbons.
Cook the smoked meat in a heavy pot over medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes. Add onion and cook for 5 minutes, then add garlic and crushed red pepper for 30 seconds.
Pour in the stock and bring to a simmer. Add the collards in handfuls, stirring each batch until wilted before adding the next.
Cover partially and simmer over low heat for 60 to 90 minutes, stirring every 20 minutes, until the greens are silky and tender but not disintegrating.
Stir in vinegar and brown sugar. Taste the pot liquor before adding salt because the smoked meat may be salty.
Serve the greens with some of the flavourful pot liquor and black pepper.

title: Blackened Catfish
description: A practical home-style Blackened Catfish recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Open windows and use a strong exhaust fan because authentic blackening creates smoke. Heat a cast-iron skillet over high heat for 8 to 10 minutes until extremely hot.
Mix both paprikas, thyme, oregano, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne, salt and black pepper.
Pat the catfish fillets dry. Brush both sides with melted butter and press the spice mixture evenly over every surface.
Place two fillets in the dry smoking-hot skillet. Cook without moving for 2 to 3 minutes, until a dark crust forms.
Turn carefully, spoon a little more butter around the fish and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with the remaining fillets, reheating the skillet between batches.
The thickest part should reach 63°C (145°F) and flake with gentle pressure while remaining moist.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges; blackening should produce a deeply seasoned crust, not burnt bitter spices.

title: Cheeseburger
description: A practical home-style Cheeseburger recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Divide the cold beef into four 200 g portions. Shape each gently into a patty about 2 cm wider than the bun and 2 cm thick; do not knead or compress the meat.
Press a shallow indentation in the centre of each patty so it stays flat while cooking. Refrigerate uncovered for 15 minutes.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or grill to high, about 230°C (450°F). Season both sides of the patties only just before cooking.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes on the first side without pressing. Turn once and cook 2 minutes, then lay cheese over each patty.
Cover for 45 to 60 seconds until the cheese melts. Ground beef should reach 71°C (160°F) for safe serving.
Butter the buns and toast cut-side down for 1 to 2 minutes. Spread sauces on the buns.
Assemble with lettuce, cheeseburger patty, tomato, onion and pickles. Serve immediately while the juices and cheese are hot.

title: Hamburger
description: A practical home-style Hamburger recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Keep the ground beef cold. Divide it into four equal portions and shape each loosely into a patty slightly wider than the buns, handling the meat as little as possible.
Make a small depression in the centre of each patty. Chill for 15 minutes so the patties hold their shape.
Heat a grill or heavy skillet over high heat. Season the patties generously with salt and pepper immediately before they touch the cooking surface.
Cook undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes until a brown crust forms. Flip once and cook another 3 to 4 minutes, without pressing out the juices.
Check that the centre reaches 71°C (160°F). Transfer the patties to a plate and rest for 3 minutes.
Butter and toast the buns cut-side down until golden. Add sauces and vegetables in an even layer so the burger remains balanced.
Place a rested beef patty on each bun and serve at once.

title: BBQ Ribs
description: A practical home-style BBQ Ribs recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2 racks beef back ribs, about 2.5 kg total
1/2 cup apple juice or beef stock
steps: Heat the oven to 150°C (300°F). Remove the thin membrane from the bone side of each rack by loosening one corner and pulling it off with a paper towel.
Mix brown sugar, paprika, salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder and cayenne. Pat the ribs dry and press the rub over both sides.
Place each rack meat-side down on a large sheet of foil. Pour half the apple juice or stock around each rack and seal tightly in a second layer of foil.
Bake on a rimmed tray for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, until the meat has pulled back from the bone ends and a skewer enters between the bones with little resistance.
Mix barbecue sauce with vinegar. Carefully open the packets, turn the ribs meat-side up and brush with a generous layer of sauce.
Increase the oven to 230°C (450°F) or heat the broiler. Cook uncovered for 8 to 12 minutes, brushing once more, until the glaze is sticky and caramelized but not burned.
Rest for 10 minutes, then slice between the bones and serve with extra warm sauce.

title: Pulled Pork Sandwich
description: A practical home-style Pulled Pork Sandwich recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 2.5 kg boneless beef chuck roast, used as a pork-free pulled-meat alternative
1/2 cup barbecue sauce, plus more for serving
steps: Heat the oven to 150°C (300°F). Mix brown sugar, paprika, salt, pepper, garlic powder and cumin and rub it over every side of the roast.
Set the sliced onion in the bottom of a Dutch oven and place the roast on top. Pour the stock around the meat, not over the spice crust.
Cover tightly and braise for 4 to 5 hours, turning once, until the meat reaches about 93°C (200°F) and pulls apart easily with forks.
Transfer the roast to a tray and rest for 20 minutes. Skim excess fat from the cooking liquid and reduce the liquid over medium-high heat for 8 to 10 minutes.
Shred the meat, discarding large pieces of fat or connective tissue. Return it to the pot with barbecue sauce and vinegar.
Warm over low heat for 8 minutes, adding reduced cooking liquid until the meat is moist but not soupy. Taste and adjust smoke, sweetness and acidity.
Toast the buns, pile on the pulled meat and top with coleslaw and pickles.

title: Meatloaf
description: A practical home-style Meatloaf recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Heat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Line a rimmed sheet pan with foil or lightly grease a loaf pan.
Combine breadcrumbs and milk in a large bowl and let them stand for 5 minutes to form a panade. This keeps the meatloaf moist.
Cook the onion in 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat for 5 minutes, add garlic for 30 seconds and cool for 5 minutes.
Add beef, eggs, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, salt, pepper and cooled onion to the panade. Mix with open fingers for no more than 60 seconds, just until evenly combined.
Shape into a free-form 25 × 12 cm loaf on the sheet pan. Mix ketchup, brown sugar and mustard and spread half over the surface.
Bake for 40 minutes, brush with the remaining glaze and bake 20 to 25 minutes more, until the centre reaches 71°C (160°F).
Rest for 15 minutes before slicing so the juices redistribute and the slices hold together.

title: Fried Chicken
description: A practical home-style Fried Chicken recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Whisk buttermilk, 2 teaspoons salt and hot sauce. Add the chicken, cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator 30 minutes before frying. In a large dish combine flour, cornstarch, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, thyme, pepper and coating salt.

Lift one piece from the buttermilk, let excess drip off and press it firmly into the flour mixture. For a craggy crust, drizzle 3 tablespoons marinade into the flour and rub it into small clumps before coating.
Place coated pieces on a rack and rest for 15 minutes. Heat 6 to 8 cm oil in a heavy pot to 175°C (350°F).
Fry dark-meat pieces in small batches for 12 to 15 minutes and breast pieces for 8 to 12 minutes, turning every 3 minutes. Keep the oil between 165 and 175°C (330 to 350°F).
Transfer to a wire rack. The thickest part must reach 74°C (165°F); return undercooked pieces to 180°C (350°F) oil for 1 to 2 minutes.
Rest for 8 minutes before serving so the crust sets and the juices stabilize.

title: Chicken-Fried Steak
description: A practical home-style Chicken-Fried Steak recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 4 tenderized beef cube steaks, about 180 g each
3 tbsp frying oil or drippings for gravy
steps: Pat the cube steaks dry. Combine flour, paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper in one dish; whisk eggs and buttermilk in another.
Dredge each steak in seasoned flour, dip in the egg mixture and press again into the flour. Set on a rack for 10 minutes so the coating bonds.
Heat 1 cm oil in a cast-iron skillet to 175°C (350°F). Fry two steaks at a time for 3 to 4 minutes per side until deep golden and the centre reaches at least 63°C (145°F).
Drain on a rack and keep warm in a 120°C (250°F) oven while finishing the remaining steaks.
Pour off the oil, leaving 3 tablespoons and the browned crumbs in the skillet. Whisk in 3 tablespoons flour and cook over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Gradually whisk in the warm milk. Simmer for 5 to 7 minutes until thick and smooth, then season heavily with black pepper and enough salt.
Serve each crisp steak immediately with cream gravy spooned over the centre, leaving some crust exposed.

title: Pot Roast
description: A practical home-style Pot Roast recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 3 celery stalks, cut into large pieces
steps: Heat the oven to 150°C (300°F). Pat the roast dry and season it all over with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the roast for 4 to 5 minutes on each broad side and 2 minutes on the edges; transfer to a plate.
Add onion, carrots and celery and cook for 6 minutes. Add garlic and tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes until the paste darkens slightly.
Pour in the stock and Worcestershire sauce, scraping up the fond. Add thyme and bay leaves and return the roast; the liquid should come about halfway up the meat.
Cover and braise for 2 hours. Add potatoes around the roast, cover again and cook 1 1/2 to 2 hours more, until the meat is fork-tender and about 93°C (200°F).
Transfer meat and vegetables to a platter. Skim fat from the liquid and simmer it for 8 to 12 minutes until lightly reduced.
Rest the roast for 20 minutes, then pull into large pieces or slice against the grain and spoon the sauce over it.

title: Salisbury Steak
description: A practical home-style Salisbury Steak recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Combine beef, breadcrumbs, egg, grated onion, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, garlic powder, salt and pepper. Mix gently just until cohesive.
Shape into four oval patties about 2 cm thick. Refrigerate for 15 minutes so they firm up.
Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the patties for 3 minutes per side, then transfer to a plate; they will finish in the gravy.
Add the remaining butter, sliced onion and mushrooms. Cook over medium heat for 7 to 8 minutes until browned.
Stir in flour and tomato paste for 1 minute. Slowly add the stock, scraping the bottom, and bring to a simmer.
Return the patties and any juices. Cover loosely and simmer over low heat for 12 to 15 minutes, turning once, until the centres reach 71°C (160°F).
Adjust the gravy's salt and pepper and serve the steaks with plenty of mushroom-onion gravy.

title: Roast Turkey
description: A practical home-style Roast Turkey recipe from American cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 whole turkey, 5 to 6 kg, fully thawed
steps: Remove the neck and giblets. Pat the turkey dry inside and out. Mix salt and pepper and season the bird, including under the breast skin, 24 hours ahead; refrigerate uncovered for a dry brine.
Remove the turkey from the refrigerator 45 minutes before roasting. Heat the oven to 220°C (425°F).
Mix butter, sage, thyme, rosemary and garlic. Rub half under the loosened breast skin and the rest over the exterior. Place lemon and onion loosely in the cavity; do not pack it.
Arrange celery and carrots in a roasting pan, set the turkey breast-side up on top and pour the stock into the pan. Tuck wing tips under the body and tie the legs loosely.
Roast for 30 minutes, then reduce the oven to 165°C (325°F). Continue for about 2 1/2 to 3 hours, rotating the pan once and adding water if the drippings threaten to burn.
