Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add the beans in batches and stir-fry for 6 to 8 minutes until wrinkled, blistered and crisp-tender; remove.
Add the remaining oil and cook the ground pork for 3 to 4 minutes until browned, or cook mushrooms until their liquid evaporates.
Add garlic, ginger, dried chillies and preserved mustard greens, if using, and stir-fry for 20 to 30 seconds.
Return the beans and add the light soy sauce and sugar.
Toss over high heat for 1 to 2 minutes until the seasoning is absorbed and the wok is dry.
Drizzle with sesame oil and taste before adding any salt because the preserved greens and soy sauce are already salty.
Serve immediately while the beans retain a slight bite.

title: Buddha’s Delight
description: A practical home-style Buddha’s Delight recipe from Chinese cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 3 tbsp water
steps: Press the tofu for 15 minutes, cube it and pat it dry. Cut all vegetables into similar bite-size pieces and keep firmer and tender vegetables separate.
Whisk the light soy sauce, oyster or mushroom sauce, sugar, cornstarch slurry and 1/2 cup water.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Brown the tofu for 5 to 6 minutes, turning carefully, then remove it.
Add the remaining oil and garlic. Stir for 10 seconds, then add carrot, broccoli and baby corn and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add mushrooms and napa cabbage and cook for another 2 minutes. Add snow peas last so they remain crisp.
Return the tofu, stir the sauce and pour it around the vegetables. Bring to a simmer.
Cover for 1 to 2 minutes, then uncover and toss until the sauce is glossy and the vegetables are crisp-tender.
Finish with sesame oil and serve at once with steamed rice.

title: Stir-Fried Chinese Cabbage
description: A practical home-style Stir-Fried Chinese Cabbage recipe from Chinese cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Separate the napa cabbage leaves, wash and dry them. Cut the thick white stems into 3 cm pieces and the leafy tops into larger pieces; keep them separate.
Mix the light soy sauce, rice vinegar and sugar in a small bowl.
Heat a wok over high heat and add the neutral oil. Add dried chillies and stir for 5 to 10 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced garlic and the cabbage stems. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until the stems begin to turn translucent at the edges.
Add the leafy tops and toss for 60 to 90 seconds until just wilted.
Pour the prepared sauce around the hot edge of the wok and toss for 30 seconds.
Cook only until excess moisture has evaporated; the cabbage should remain juicy and slightly crisp rather than soft.
Finish with sesame oil, taste for salt and serve immediately.

title: Ma Po Tofu—Mild Version
description: A practical home-style Ma Po Tofu—Mild Version recipe from Chinese cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
steps: Cut the tofu into 2 cm cubes and simmer it in lightly salted water for 2 minutes. Drain gently and keep warm.
Mix the soy sauce, sugar, stock and mild chilli bean sauce in a bowl. Stir the cornstarch slurry separately just before use.
Heat a wok over medium-high heat and cook the minced chicken for 4 to 5 minutes until no pink remains, or sauté mushrooms until browned and dry.
Add garlic and ginger and stir for 20 seconds, then pour in the seasoned stock and bring it to a simmer.
Slide in the tofu and simmer gently for 4 minutes. Shake the wok or nudge the cubes with a spatula instead of stirring vigorously.
Add the cornstarch slurry in two additions, simmering after each, until the sauce lightly coats the tofu.
Taste and adjust the mild chilli bean sauce or soy sauce without making the dish overly salty.
Scatter with spring onions and serve hot with rice.

title: Spinach with Garlic Sauce
description: A practical home-style Spinach with Garlic Sauce recipe from Chinese cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
steps: Wash the spinach in several changes of cold water, then spin or pat it as dry as possible. Keep the tender stems attached and cut only very thick stems.
Mix the light soy sauce, sugar and water until the sugar dissolves.
Heat a wok over high heat until very hot. Add the neutral oil and sliced garlic and stir for 8 to 10 seconds.
Add the spinach stems and half the leaves first. Toss continuously for 30 seconds, then add the remaining leaves.
Pour in the soy mixture and continue tossing for 30 to 60 seconds until all leaves are just wilted and bright green.
Move the spinach to a serving plate immediately so residual heat does not overcook it.
Drizzle with sesame oil and spoon any garlicky juices from the wok over the top.
Serve at once; the finished spinach should be tender but not watery or dull.

title: Szechuan Eggplant
description: A practical home-style Szechuan Eggplant recipe from Chinese cuisine
licenseNote: Editorial home-kitchen formulation generated for this catalogue; test and review for local ingredient standards, nutrition, food safety, and allergen compliance before commercial publication.
ingredients: 700 g Chinese eggplant, cut into batons
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 3 tbsp water
steps: Cut the Chinese eggplant into even batons. Toss with 1 teaspoon salt, rest for 15 minutes and pat dry thoroughly.
Whisk the light soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, cornstarch slurry and 1/3 cup water.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Brown the eggplant in batches for 5 to 7 minutes until tender and lightly blistered; remove.
Add the remaining oil and cook the ground pork for 3 to 4 minutes until browned, or cook mushrooms until dry.
Add garlic, ginger and chilli bean paste and stir-fry for 30 seconds until the oil turns fragrant and red.
Return the eggplant, stir the sauce and pour it into the wok. Toss gently to avoid breaking the softened pieces.
Simmer for 1 to 2 minutes until the sauce thickens and clings to the eggplant.
Finish with spring onions and serve immediately with steamed rice.
